title: hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. castellae
intro: Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. castellae is a subspecies of the Hieracium chondrillifolium plant, a type of hawkweed that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of gastrointestinal issues, due to its bitter compounds and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and boosted immune system. The herbal preparations of Hieracium chondrillifolium subsp. castellae include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
